purpose: Defines the `!history [--last N] [--analytics]` command for viewing past swarm history summaries. History is GLOBAL across all dashboards (no dashboardId param) and is populated when a dashboard is cleared (via Clear Dashboard button or !reset). Lists each history JSON file from {tracker_root}/history/, sorts by cleared_at descending, and renders a table. The --analytics subcommand additionally computes aggregate metrics across all history (avg tasks/duration, failure rate trend, largest/longest swarm, top failure-keyword patterns) and writes them to history/analytics.json.
gotchas: History is global — no dashboardId argument; do not auto-detect a dashboard for this command
History summaries are produced by the server's buildHistorySummary() function — schema must match what HistoryService.js writes
--analytics persists results to {tracker_root}/history/analytics.json (this is the ONLY history command write — base !history is read-only)
failure_rate_trend compares LAST 3 swarms against the overall rate (improving/stable/degrading)
failure_patterns groups failed agents by FIRST significant word in their title — this is a heuristic and may misgroup
Status emojis: completed=✅, completed_with_errors=⚠️, failed=🔴, in_progress=🔵 (last shouldn't normally appear in history)
patterns: 12-step procedure: read dir → handle empty → parse each → sort → apply --last → render table → compute summary; analytics adds steps 8-12
Analytics computes 8 distinct metrics + failure_patterns array
conventions: Note callout that history requires no dashboardId param
Two output sections: main listing table + (if --analytics) analytics report table with Failure Patterns sub-table
